title: oxalis acetosella var. japonica
intro: Oxalis acetosella var. japonica, also known as Japanese wood sorrel or mountain sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). It is a perennial plant native to Japan, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and edible leaves with a tangy, sour taste. Oxalis acetosella var. japonica has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as digestive issues, urinary tract infections, and skin irritations. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by infusing its leaves in hot water or mixing its extracts with other herbs to create a range of remedies.
